title: Usability: Support building an AppImage
body:
Verilator is still constantly improving and adding great features, and is getting increasingly supported by virtually all open source IP, there still is the issue that people may be offset by having to build verilator regularly :) Also, adding a particular version to various CI flows is a bit of an "issue".
The run-docker container was a first approach, but it suffers from a couple of issues (docker requirement may be bad for CI, not arbitrary files in the system can be used).
I therefore had a brief look into portable alternatives. The most widespread are snap, flatpak and AppImage. I have no expertise on those, but AppImage seems a decent candidate. Shortly summarized it packs a portable installed version of a program into a ramdisk, puts that into an elf, which then at runtime fuse-mounts this into /tmp. This works quite well.
There are a couple of things to check/implement:

- [ ] I assume we need the ability for a static (fat) build
- [ ] An installation must be relocatable
- [ ] The runtime library must be copied into the generated output. The docker image does this too, will open an issue to support this generally as I think it has good value beyond this issue.
- [ ] Bring up AppImage build
- [ ] Continuous deployment of AppImage, so that people can use automatically generated builds

Especially for the first two I am halfway confident that they are mostly done, but I will now start playing around.
Do you like the idea, @wsnyder?
